sources_exist.note: All four URL-bearing citation occurrences resolve across two unique SHRM pages; each occurrence was audited at its exact citations[0].url path.

figures_match_sources.note: The cited pages state the quoted 19.1%, 43%/26%, and 51% figures verbatim and provide the stated US survey/OEWS context; the citation scopes candidly disclose that these are HR occupation or broad-employer evidence rather than NAICS 541612 firm evidence.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: Most values and estimate ranges are candid and plausible, but inputs.historical_analogs is labeled DIRECT while its value adds an uncited conclusion about replacement of fee-based HR consulting that the recruiting source does not establish.

rubric_consistent.note: Template/finalizer identity, deterministic dataset equality, supplied SOC/title/wage-share identity, residual arithmetic, t50 boundary, EBITDA units and owner-comp normalization, pricing deflation, zero/low-count search framing, and the frozen rubric structure are consistent.

cross_checks_honest.note: The cross-checks separate operator savings from product replacement, keep deal evidence out of V/A, map effort-based repricing into pi_dist/pi_moat, acknowledge the 4-digit role-mix mismatch and heterogeneity, and do not conceal the estimate-heavy evidence base.

source_audits.note: The live page states that at least 50% of tasks are automated in 19.1% of HR jobs and separately reports 27.2% for compensation, benefits, and job analysis specialists. Its methods identify a March-April 2025 survey of 20,262 US workers combined with May 2024 OEWS occupation estimates, matching the disclosed proxy scope.

The live page states that 43% of organizations leverage AI in HR tasks and reports the survey methodology as 2,040 HR professionals across US industries; the record correctly treats 35% NAICS 541612 adoption as a disclosed derived scope adjustment rather than a direct fact.

The live page states that just over half of organizations (51%) use AI to support recruiting and explains that human intelligence remains indispensable for cultural fit, soft skills, and bias mitigation. Those atomic source facts are supported, although the DIRECT input value improperly extends them to fee-based consulting.

The live page states verbatim that 43% of organizations leverage AI in HR tasks, up from 26% in 2024, and its methodology confirms a February 2025 US cross-industry HR-professional survey; the record discloses the target-industry mismatch.

reasons: inputs.historical_analogs.value and inputs.historical_analogs.provenance_type: the DIRECT value concludes that the recruiting evidence supports task assistance rather than replacement of fee-based HR consulting, but the cited page addresses organizations' internal recruiting workflows and does not state that consulting-market conclusion. Restrict the DIRECT value to facts actually stated by the source, or classify the consulting interpretation as DERIVED and provide a complete, scope-aware bridge from the recruiting facts to fee-based NAICS 541612 demand.
flags_reviewed: ESTIMATE_HEAVY: 7 inputs with provenance_type ESTIMATE (active_consolidators, buy_mult, exit_mult, owners_60plus_pct, pi_dist, pi_moat, pricing_structure)
